Document:
# MAJOR CRIMES
## Disorderly Conduct Severely disrupting colony operations, emergency response, or essential services (blocking emergency response, interfering with evacuation, causing panic during emergency operations, disrupting medical triage). - 10 min
## Assault Threatening or using physical force with hostile intent. - 10 min unarmed | 15 min with a deadly weapon
## Manslaughter Killing someone without malicious intent due to reckless or negligent conduct. - 20 min + possible license revocation, job removal, or permanent confinement if the person remains a danger
## Illegal Confinement Unlawfully restraining, kidnapping, or imprisoning another person. Does not apply to lawful quarantine, arrest, or protective custody. - 20 min
## Major Theft Stealing colony funds, emergency supplies, weapons, vehicles, access cards, or restricted equipment. - 15 min + return of property
## Unauthorized Access to Corporate Property Accessing company equipment, labs, records, or restricted areas without authorization. - 7 min (minor access) | 15 min if restricted materials, vehicles, records, or systems are involved
## Misuse of Colony Infrastructure Improperly operating, disabling, or modifying colony systems (atmosphere processors, reactor systems, airlocks, water processors, emergency shelters, fuel systems). - 15 min | 20 min if public safety endangered | Permanent confinement if mass death or colony collapse risk
## Sabotage Damaging, disabling, or compromising colony infrastructure, utilities, or safety/security systems. - 20 min | Permanent confinement if the act creates mass danger
## Possession of Restricted Contraband Possessing restricted drugs, military equipment, explosives, illegal weapons, classified material, or dangerous organisms. - 15-20 min + confiscation
## Unauthorized Distress Signal Sending a distress call, emergency beacon, or evacuation signal without lawful authorization. - 15 min | 20 min if it causes panic, military deployment, or operational disruption
## Interference with Emergency Response Obstructing marshals, medical staff, evacuation crews, or other lawful emergency responders. - 15 min | 20 min if injury, death, contamination, or major delay results
## Biohazard Noncompliance Refusing lawful quarantine, decontamination, medical screening, or isolation procedures. - 20 min | Permanent confinement if the violation risks outbreak, mass infection, or colony‑wide contamination
## Possession of Restricted Biological Material Possessing, hiding, transporting, or selling dangerous biological material without authorization (unknown eggs, parasitic organisms, pathogen samples, infected tissue, invasive lifeform remains, unknown organic material from restricted zones). - 20 min + confiscation/destruction + mandatory quarantine | Permanent confinement if colony safety endangered
## Harboring a Contaminated Person Knowingly hiding or assisting a person under quarantine or suspected of carrying a dangerous pathogen. - 20 min | Permanent confinement if outbreak risk is created
## Corporate Espionage Stealing, copying, or transmitting restricted corporate, scientific, or biological research data. - 20 min | Permanent confinement if tied to hostile powers, terrorist groups, or hazardous biological research
## Unauthorized Disclosure Releasing restricted colony, scientific, military, or biological information without authorization. - 10 min (minor) | 20 min if it causes panic or operational disruption | Permanent confinement if mass casualties or colony collapse risk
## Corruption Using public, law enforcement, or administrative authority for personal gain or unlawful punishment. - 15 min + removal from position
## Abuse of Authority Issuing unlawful arrests, searches, seizures, administrative orders, or emergency directives. - 15 min + removal from position
## Harboring a Fugitive Knowingly hiding or assisting a person wanted for a major or capital crime. - 15 min
## Rioting Participating in violent public unrest involving property damage, assault, theft, arson, or obstruction of emergency services. - 20 min
